rationale: Probes via rdm story mutation-probe on rdm/record/dmr.py, all KILLED. Clauses: (1) one entry per controlled document, an un-identified document is not indexed (and is skipped loudly): `if not doc_id:` -> `if False:` KILLED (notes.md warning on stderr + the exact entries equality); (2) entry fields generated from frontmatter - revision: `"revision": frontmatter.get("revision")` -> `None` KILLED; path: `md.relative_to(base).as_posix() ...` -> `md.name` KILLED; id and title are pinned by the same exact entries-list equality; (3) written to a data file the DMR index renders from, marked generated: dropping the GENERATED header from the written file KILLED; deterministic regeneration is asserted byte-for-byte. Documented equivalent mutant: `return sorted(entries, key=id)` -> `return entries` SURVIVED - glob filename order coincides with id order in this fixture and both are deterministic; ordering is not a clause of the DI text.
